id: c_plainContext_v4_NOISE_L40_Buying a new phone or laptop
scriptId: cs_v4_NOISE_L40_Buying a new phone or laptop
historyId: h_v4_NOISE_L40_Buying a new phone or laptop
context:
Current State:
I browsed a custom hardware shop and found some wild niche products; fun to look at but not necessary.
There is a tech fair next weekend; I might swing by to handle a few machines and see what feels right.
Checklist to test machines at the fair:
- Type for 5–10 minutes to feel key travel, layout, and palm rest comfort.
- Open/close the lid several times to test hinge smoothness and screen wobble.
- Lift and carry the laptop as you would on your commute to judge true weight/balance.
- Look at the screen in person for brightness, glare, and viewing angles.
- Check ports (charger, USB‑C, HDMI, SD) so you don’t end up needing too many adapters.
- Ask about battery life and warranty; for refurbished units, ask to see battery health and confirm the previous owner’s account is removed.
- Confirm the seller’s return window and repair/coverage options in case you decide it’s not right after a day or two.
The assistant offered to turn that into a one‑page printable checklist to bring to the fair.
